title: World population reaches 1 billion
detail: It took all of human history to reach this milestone. The next billion would take just 127 years.

title: Luddites smash machines
detail: Workers in England destroy textile machinery, fearing automation will take their jobs. The first clash between progress and people.

title: Year Without a Summer
detail: Mount Tambora's eruption blankets the globe in ash. Crop failures trigger famine across Europe and North America.

title: First cholera pandemic reaches Europe
detail: The disease kills tens of thousands. It will take decades before germ theory replaces miasma as the accepted explanation.

title: Communist Manifesto published
detail: Marx and Engels write: 'The history of all hitherto existing society is the history of class struggles.' It will reshape the century.

title: Nightingale goes to Crimea
detail: Florence Nightingale transforms military medicine. Her statistical charts will prove that cleanliness saves more lives than combat.

title: On the Origin of Species
detail: Darwin publishes the theory of evolution by natural selection. It will take a century for the full implications to sink in.

title: Bell patents the telephone
detail: Alexander Graham Bell's invention will, within a century, connect every person on Earth.

title: Edison opens Pearl Street Station
detail: The first commercial power station lights lower Manhattan. The age of electricity begins.

title: X-rays discovered
detail: Wilhelm Roentgen sees through flesh for the first time. Medicine will never be the same.

title: First powered flight
detail: The Wright brothers fly for 12 seconds at Kitty Hawk. Within 66 years, humans will walk on the Moon.

title: Spanish Flu kills 50 million
detail: More than any war. The pandemic kills more people in 24 weeks than HIV/AIDS killed in 24 years.

title: Penicillin discovered
detail: Alexander Fleming notices a mold killing bacteria. Antibiotics will save an estimated 200 million lives.

title: World War II begins
detail: The deadliest conflict in human history. 70-85 million dead. The world will never again see war on this scale.

title: United Nations founded
detail: From the ruins of war, 51 nations pledge to 'save succeeding generations from the scourge of war.' The modern international order begins.

title: Universal Declaration of Human Rights
detail: Eleanor Roosevelt calls it 'a Magna Carta for all mankind.' For the first time, the world agrees on what every person deserves.

title: DNA structure discovered
detail: Watson and Crick reveal the double helix. The code of life is cracked.

title: Green Revolution begins
detail: Norman Borlaug's high-yield wheat varieties will save an estimated billion lives from famine.

title: Civil Rights Act signed
detail: Martin Luther King Jr. pushes America toward its own ideals. The law now says what morality always should have.

title: Moon landing
detail: Neil Armstrong takes one small step. 600 million people watch live. The impossible becomes routine.

title: Club of Rome warns of limits
detail: 'Limits to Growth' predicts ecological collapse if trends continue. The world largely ignores the warning.

title: Smallpox eradicated
detail: The only human disease ever completely wiped out. A triumph of global cooperation.

title: HIV/AIDS identified
detail: The epidemic will kill 40 million people. It will take decades and unprecedented drug access programs to bring it under control.

title: World Wide Web goes public
detail: Tim Berners-Lee makes the internet free for everyone. Information, for the first time, has no gatekeeper.

title: Millennium Development Goals
detail: 189 nations commit to 8 goals: halve poverty, achieve universal education, stop HIV/AIDS. It is the largest anti-poverty effort in history.

title: Human Genome Project completed
detail: Every gene in the human body is mapped. The blueprint of our species is written down.

title: Extreme poverty halves
detail: The MDG target for poverty is met 5 years early. 1 billion people lifted out of extreme poverty since 1990.

title: Sustainable Development Goals adopted
detail: 17 goals, 169 targets, one deadline: 2030. The most ambitious plan humanity has ever agreed to.

title: COVID-19 pandemic
detail: Global life expectancy drops for the first time in decades. But vaccines are developed in record time — 327 days from sequence to injection.

title: Child mortality reaches historic low
detail: 35 per 1,000, down from 460 in 1800. The greatest improvement in human welfare ever recorded.
